Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 61 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 2.8x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 61 actions returned the board to a state already seen 61 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 61 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The game says "The game is over" but I'm still on level 1. The blob got stuck at (40, 31). Looking at the image, I can see the blob is now further from G than before. The trail has changed direction.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics from scratch:
1. There's a ring at the top
2. There's a G target at the bottom
3. Clicking the ring spawns a blob that moves toward G
4. The blob moves along a white trail
The key insight I might be missing: maybe clicking the ring at different positions changes where the blob spawns or which direction it moves. Or maybe I need to click specific points on the ring to guide the blob's path.
Looking at the image, the ring is at the top and the blob is now at a position where it got stuck. The G target is below. Maybe I need to click the ring at a position that aligns with G, so the blob moves directly to it.
Let me try clicking different parts of the ring to see if the blob's trajectory changes. The ring center is around (21, 39) but G is at column ~29. Maybe I need to click the left side of the ring to make the blob go left toward G.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game is "over" but still on level 1. Let me check the state.
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current objects:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('M', 'G', 'p'):
        b = n['boundary']
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b), sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b))
        print(f"  {n['color']}: center={center}, px={n['pixels']}")

# Check if there's a game_over flag
print(f"\nlast_action_result: {last_action_result}")

# The ring is at top, G is at bottom
# Ring center ~ (21, 39), G center ~ (47, 29)
# Maybe I need to click the ring at a position aligned with G
# G is to the left of the ring center, so click left side of ring

# Ring spans roughly cols 36-42, rows 18-24
# Try clicking the leftmost part of the ring
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 36}])

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
nodes2 = seg2['nodes']
print("\nAfter clicking ring left:")
for n in nodes2:
    if n['color'] in ('M', 'G'):
        b = n['boundary']
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b), sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b))
        print(f"  {n['color']}: center={center}, px={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
